+{"id":"bd-jrjwx","title":"Bug: resolve_conflicts.go has closed winning over tombstone (should be reversed)","description":"In `cmd/bd/resolve_conflicts.go` lines 418-425:

```go
// Status: closed wins
if left.Status == "closed" || right.Status == "closed" {
    result.Status = "closed"
} else if left.Status == "tombstone" || right.Status == "tombstone" {
    result.Status = "tombstone"
}
```

This is backwards. Tombstone should win over closed because:
1. Tombstone represents an explicit deletion
2. The main merge library (`internal/merge/merge.go` lines 657-660) correctly has tombstone winning first
3. This bug causes corrupted state: status=closed + deleted_at set

**Fix:** Swap the order so tombstone is checked first.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:26:17.808709-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:26:17.808709-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-jsk7","title":"Agent beads: structured labels for filtering","description":"## Agent Beads Need Queryable Labels

Currently agent beads have role_type/rig in description text, not as labels. This breaks @group resolution in gt mail.

## Current State
```json
{
  "id": "gt-gastown-witness",
  "issue_type": "agent",
  "description": "...\\nrole_type: witness\\nrig: gastown\\n..."
}
```

Cannot query: `bd list --type=agent --label=role_type:witness` returns nothing.

## Required
Agent bead creation should add labels:
- `role_type:<type>` (witness, refinery, crew, polecat, dog, mayor, deacon)
- `rig:<rig>` (gastown, beads, or "town" for town-level)

## Where to Fix
gt polecat/crew/agent creation commands should add labels.

## Acceptance
- New agent beads created with role_type/rig labels
- @group patterns work in gt mail router","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T20:49:13.444793-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-29T20:58:04.67866-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T20:58:04.67866-08:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-g7eq"}
 {"id":"bd-jv4w","title":"Phase 1.2: Separate bdt executable - Initial structure","description":"Create minimal bdt command structure completely separate from bd. Must not share code, config, or database.

## Subtasks
1. Create cmd/bdt/ directory with main.go
2. Implement bdt version, help, and status commands
3. Configure separate database location: $HOME/.bdt/ (not $HOME/.beads/)
4. Create separate issues file: issues.toon (not issues.jsonl)
5. Update build system:
   - Makefile: Add bdt target
   - .goreleaser.yml: Add bdt binary config

## Files to Create
- cmd/bdt/main.go - Entry point
- cmd/bdt/version.go - Version handling
- cmd/bdt/help.go - Help text (separate from bd)

## Success Criteria
- `make build` produces both `bd` and `bdt` executables
- `bdt version` shows distinct version output from bd
- `bdt --help` shows distinct help text
- bdt uses $HOME/.bdt/ directory (verify with `bdt info`)
- bd and bdt completely isolated (no shared imports beyond stdlib)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T11:48:34.866877282-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:59:11.389296015-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:59:11.389296015-07:00"}

 {"id":"bd-mfrs6","title":"gt doctor priming check: AGENTS.md check misses rig-level files","description":"The AGENTS.md > 20 lines check is only in checkAgentPriming(), which checks mayor, deacon, witness, refinery directories. But rig-level AGENTS.md files (e.g., gastown/AGENTS.md, beads/AGENTS.md) are not checked.

These rig-level files should also be thin bootstrap pointers.

Fix: Add AGENTS.md check to checkRigPriming() similar to how PRIME.md is checked.

Location: gastown/mayor/rig/internal/doctor/priming_check.go:152-164","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/polecats/rictus","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:46:13.923911-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:03:23.774713-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:03:23.774713-08:00","close_reason":"Added AGENTS.md size check to checkRigPriming() in priming_check.go"}
+{"id":"bd-mfube","title":"Add bd doctor check for non-tombstone issues with deleted_at","description":"Add a doctor check to detect invalid issue states where:
- status is NOT 'tombstone'
- deleted_at IS set

This is an invariant violation that can cause sync failures with error:
'non-tombstone issues cannot have deleted_at timestamp'

The check should:
1. Query for issues with this invalid state
2. Report the count and example IDs
3. Suggest fix: either convert to tombstones or remove deleted_at

Implementation location: `cmd/bd/doctor/integrity.go`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:26:18.019656-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:26:18.019656-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-mhhb","title":"Read operations should not modify database file","description":"Read-only operations (bd list, bd ready, bd show) modify the database file. Every open sets WAL pragma, runs schema/migrations, and checkpoints on close. Need sqlite.NewReadOnly() that opens with mode=ro, skips WAL/schema/migrations, and skips checkpoint. See GH#804.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T16:27:13.416643-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-30T16:46:49.921614-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T16:46:49.921614-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented read-only mode for SQLite. Read commands now use NewReadOnly() which opens with mode=ro, skips WAL pragma/schema/migrations, and skips checkpoint on close."}
